explanation: The property states that globally, if the ventilator is in PSV mode and the breathing time is greater than or equal to the apnea lag time (indicating no breath triggered within the window), then in the next state, the ventilator shall switch to PCV mode and set the respiratory rate, inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio to their apnea backup values. This accurately captures the requirement for apnea backup ventilation in PSV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is currently in PSV Mode (bool_exp1) AND the breathing time has exceeded or is equal to the apnea lag time (bool_exp2), which indicates the patient has not triggered a breath within the allowed window, THEN in the very next state (X), the ventilator shall be in PCV Mode AND its respiratory rate (RR), inspiratory pressure (P_insp), and inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio (I:E) shall be set to their respective apnea backup values (bool_exp3).

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV mode and the breathing time is greater than or equal to the apnea lag time (indicating the patient did not trigger a breath within the apnea window), then in the next state (X), the ventilator shall be in PCV mode with the respiratory rate, inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio set to their respective apnea backup values. This directly translates the 'If condition, then next state must be this' requirement.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV mode and the patient does not trigger a breath within the specified apnea lag time (indicated by 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' becoming true), then eventually the ventilator must switch to PCV mode with the respiratory rate set to RR_AP, the inspiratory pressure set to P_inspAP, and the inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio set to I:E_AP. This directly captures the requirement for apnea backup ventilation in PSV mode.

The property states that globally, if the system is currently in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) Mode and the breathing timer indicates that a patient has not triggered a new breath within the allowed apnea lag time, then in the very next state, the ventilator shall be in PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) Mode, and its respiratory rate (RR), inspiratory pressure (P_insp), and inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio (I:E) shall be set to their respective apnea backup values (RR_AP, P_inspAP, I:E_AP). This captures the requirement for apnea backup ventilation in PSV mode.

The property states that globally, if the system is currently in PSV mode AND the breathing time has exceeded or reached the apnea lag time (indicating the patient has not triggered a breath within the window), then in the next state, the ventilator shall switch to PCV mode AND the respiratory rate shall be set to RR_AP AND the inspiratory pressure shall be set to P_inspAP AND the inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio shall be set to I:E_AP.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV mode (PSVMode) and apnea is detected (apnea, which implicitly means the breath timer is greater than or equal to the apnea lag time), then eventually (F) the ventilator shall switch to PCV mode (PCVModeSelected) and its parameters (respiratory rate RR_AP, inspiratory pressure P_inspAP, and I:E ratio I:E_AP) shall be set accordingly. This is captured by the form G(A -> FB).

The property states that globally, if the system is currently in PSV mode and apnea is detected (i.e., the patient has not triggered a breath within the apnea trigger window), then in the very next state, the system shall switch to PCV mode, and simultaneously, the respiratory rate, inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio shall be set to their respective apnea backup values (RR_AP, P_inspAP, and I:E_AP). The 'X' (Next) operator precisely captures the 'shall switch to' aspect, indicating the consequence must hold immediately in the subsequent state.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV Mode and the breathing time exceeds or equals the apnea lag time (indicating no breath triggered within the window), then eventually the ventilator shall switch to PCV Mode, and its respiratory rate, inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio shall be set to their respective apnea backup values. This directly translates the 'if condition then eventually consequence' structure of the natural language.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV mode and the patient has not triggered a breath within the apnea trigger window (indicated by the breathing timer exceeding the apnea lag time), then in the very next state, the ventilator shall transition to PCV mode, and its specific apnea backup parameters (respiratory rate, inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio) will be set. The 'X' (Next) operator indicates an immediate transition to the new state where these conditions hold.
